meta.title: GCP-MLE-6: Vertex AI Production ML, Pipelines, Monitoring, and Responsible AI

domains.name: Monitoring, Optimizing, and Maintaining ML Solutions

questions.stem:
A product analyst has a clean tabular dataset and needs a baseline classification model quickly with minimal custom code for `support-chat-prod`.

The team is doing this after a 40% latency increase in production, without changing the approved data boundary, while preserving audit evidence for the release review, after a failed canary exposed missing rollback signals.

Which Vertex AI approach should the ML engineer start with?

options.text: Write a custom distributed PyTorch training loop first
Deploy the raw BigQuery table directly to an endpoint
Use Vertex AI AutoML for tabular classification
Configure Model Monitoring before any model exists

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it solves a different lifecycle step and does not satisfy the stated runtime requirement. In this scenario, choosing `Write` would not meet the required behavior for `support-chat-prod`. See [Vertex AI AutoML overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/beginner/beginners-guide).

**B** is incorrect because it would add operational work while leaving the documented service capability unused. In this scenario, choosing `Deploy` would not meet the required behavior for `support-chat-prod`. See [Vertex AI AutoML overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/beginner/beginners-guide).

**C** is correct because it provides the required managed workflow while preserving the operational and governance requirements. The option aligns with `support-chat-prod` and the constraints stated in the scenario. See [Vertex AI AutoML overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/beginner/beginners-guide).

**D** is incorrect because it targets storage, ingestion, or monitoring rather than the decision point being tested. In this scenario, choosing `Configure` would not meet the required behavior for `support-chat-prod`. See [Vertex AI AutoML overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/beginner/beginners-guide).
